learn.title: Gumax RF — learn from remote
learn.description: Select the ESPHome node and click Next. You will then have 10 seconds to press a button on your remote.

data_description.esphome_node: Select the 433 MHz capable ESPHome node that will receive the signal.

learn_wait.title: Waiting for signal…
learn_wait.description:
**{remaining}** seconds remaining

Press any button on your Gumax remote.

Captured device IDs:
{log}

data_description.device_id: Select a captured ID or type one manually.

capture_k1.title: Calibration: channel K1
capture_k1.description:
Click Next, then press any button on **channel 1 (K1)** of your remote. You have 10 seconds.

{progress}

capture_k1_wait.title: Waiting for K1 signal…
capture_k1_wait.description:
**{remaining}** seconds remaining

Press a button on channel 1 (K1).

capture_k2.title: Calibration: channel K2
capture_k2.description:
Click Next, then press any button on **channel 2 (K2)** of your remote. You have 10 seconds.

{progress}

capture_k2_wait.title: Waiting for K2 signal…
capture_k2_wait.description:
**{remaining}** seconds remaining

Press a button on channel 2 (K2).

capture_k9.title: Calibration: channel K9
capture_k9.description:
Click Next, then press any button on **channel 9 (K9)** of your remote. You have 10 seconds.

{progress}

capture_k9_wait.title: Waiting for K9 signal…
capture_k9_wait.description:
**{remaining}** seconds remaining

Press a button on channel 9 (K9).

calibration_share.title: Calibration successful
calibration_share.description:
**Validation:**
{verify_block}

Calibration succeeded and is already in use.

Want to help improve the checksum calculation for other remotes? Copy the block below and post it in [this GitHub Discussion]({discussion_url}). This is entirely optional — nothing is sent automatically, and this block contains a code that can control your own device, so only share it if you're fine with that.

```
{share_block}
```

Click Next to continue.

calibration_mismatch.title: Calibration: unexpected result
calibration_mismatch.description:
**Validation:**
{verify_block}

The calculated calibration doesn't reproduce what your remote actually sent. That points to a bug in this integration, not a problem with your remote or your input.

Please report this with the block below via [a new GitHub issue]({issue_url}). The calculated calibration is applied in the meantime, so your device keeps working.

```
{share_block}
```

Click Next to continue.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Gumax RF
reconfigure.description:
Current ESPHome node: **{current_node}**

Select the new node if you have renamed it.

data_description.esphome_node: Select the new 433 MHz capable ESPHome node.

prefix.title: Channel name prefix
prefix.description:
Set a prefix for channel names (max. 12 characters). CC always stays CC.

Channel 1 will be named: **{example}**

data_description.channel_prefix: E.g. 'C' → C1, C2 … or 'Screen' → Screen1, Screen2 …

error.invalid_device_id: Invalid hex value (max 8 characters, e.g. A1B2C3D4)
error.no_signal_received: No signal received within 10 seconds. Please try again.

abort.already_configured: This device ID is already configured.
abort.no_esphome_nodes: No ESPHome node with 433 MHz support found. Make sure your CC1101 bridge is online and try again.

configure_prefix.title: Channel name prefix
configure_prefix.description:
Set a custom prefix for channel names (max. 12 characters). CC always stays CC.

Channel 1 will be named: **{example}**

data_description.channel_prefix: E.g. 'C' → C1, C2 … or 'Screen' → Screen1, Screen2 …

view_codes.title: View raw pulse timings
view_codes.description: Select a channel to view the raw pulse timings for all commands.

capture_signal.title: Capture and analyse signal
capture_signal.description: Click Next, then press a button on your Gumax remote **multiple times**. You have 10 seconds. Only a recognisable Gumax signal will be accepted.

capture_signal_wait.title: Waiting for signal…
capture_signal_wait.description:
**{remaining}** seconds remaining

Press a button on your Gumax remote **multiple times**.

capture_signal_result.title: Received signal

error.invalid_prefix_too_long: Prefix may be at most 12 characters.
error.no_signal_received: No signal received within 10 seconds. Please try again.

init.title: Start calibration
init.description:
This integration used to apply one checksum formula to every remote — that formula is not correct for every device. **{title}** has not been calibrated yet.

Click Next to run the calibration steps (capturing K1, K2, K9). Until then, this integration keeps using the old formula.

capture_k1.title: Calibration: channel K1
capture_k1.description:
Click Next, then press any button on **channel 1 (K1)** of your remote. You have 10 seconds.

{progress}

capture_k1_wait.title: Waiting for K1 signal…
capture_k1_wait.description:
**{remaining}** seconds remaining

Press a button on channel 1 (K1).

capture_k2.title: Calibration: channel K2
capture_k2.description:
Click Next, then press any button on **channel 2 (K2)** of your remote. You have 10 seconds.

{progress}

capture_k2_wait.title: Waiting for K2 signal…
capture_k2_wait.description:
**{remaining}** seconds remaining

Press a button on channel 2 (K2).

capture_k9.title: Calibration: channel K9
capture_k9.description:
Click Next, then press any button on **channel 9 (K9)** of your remote. You have 10 seconds.

{progress}

capture_k9_wait.title: Waiting for K9 signal…
capture_k9_wait.description:
**{remaining}** seconds remaining

Press a button on channel 9 (K9).

calibration_share.title: Calibration successful
calibration_share.description:
**Validation:**
{verify_block}

Calibration succeeded and has been applied to **{title}**.

Want to help improve the checksum calculation for other remotes? Copy the block below and post it in [this GitHub Discussion]({discussion_url}). This is entirely optional — nothing is sent automatically, and this block contains a code that can control your own device, so only share it if you're fine with that.

```
{share_block}
```

Click Next to finish.

calibration_mismatch.title: Calibration: unexpected result
calibration_mismatch.description:
**Validation:**
{verify_block}

The calculated calibration doesn't reproduce what your remote actually sent. That points to a bug in this integration, not a problem with your remote or your input.

Please report this with the block below via [a new GitHub issue]({issue_url}). The calculated calibration is applied in the meantime to **{title}**, so your device keeps working.

```
{share_block}
```

Click Next to finish.
